title: APA Corporation Publishes 2022 Sustainability Report
text: HOUSTON, July 26, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- APA Corporation (Nasdaq: APA) today published its 2022 Sustainability Report, highlighting the achievement of numerous Environment, Social and Governance (ESG) goals throughout 2021 while tracking progress made on ESG commitments for 2022. The report is available here, along with a summary fact sheet detailing report highlights.

title: APA Corp.: A New Strategy Is Proposed To Realize The Full Value Of Alpine High
text: APA is undervalued based on its current operation with a free option on Alpine High and Block 58 offshore Suriname. Alpine High is a valuable asset if natural gas price trend holds.

title: APA Corporation Provides Second-Quarter 2022 Supplemental Information and Schedules Results Conference Call for August 4 at 10 a.m. Central Time
text: HOUSTON, July 18, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- APA Corporation (Nasdaq: APA) today provided supplemental information regarding certain second-quarter 2022 financial and operational results.

title: APA Corporation Board Chair John E. Lowe Announces Retirement; Director H.
text: HOUSTON, July 14, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- APA Corporation (Nasdaq: APA) today announced the election of H. Lamar McKay as the next chairman of the board. This follows the announcement by current chairman John E. Lowe of his decision to retire. Following a transition period, the change will be effective Sept. 1.

title: Can APA Corp Ride the Oil and Gas Tailwinds?
text: As the economy continues recovering from the pandemic, oil and gas prices have stabilized at relatively high levels due to the combination of travel picking back up and Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Fossil fuel companies have profited enormously from this surge in demand.

title: Is Most-Watched Stock APA Corporation (APA) Worth Betting on Now?
text: APA (APA) has been one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com users lately. So, it is worth exploring what lies ahead for the stock.

title: Oil and gas stocks suffer a broad beating as crude oil, natural gas futures are hit hard
text: The energy sector took a broad beating in afternoon trading Tuesday, as crude oil and natural gas prices took sharp dives on the back of a surge in the U.S. dollar and continued fears that a recession would dampen demand. The SPDR Energy Select Sector ETF XLE, -5.86% tumbled 5.8% toward a more than four-month low, with all 21 of the equity components losing ground by more than 4%.
